question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a stream file whose filename is fewer than 10 characters, causing streamFileData.getFilename().substring(0, 10) to throw a StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when groupByDay=true, resulting in the archive write being aborted and the record being silently dropped from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream filename whose first 10 characters contain path-traversal sequences (e.g., '../../etc/') so that getStreamPath().resolve(streamFileData.getFilename().substring(0, 10)) resolves outside the intended streams directory, causing archived records to be written to an unintended location and become missing from the expected export path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a stream filename whose first 10 bytes are a valid-looking date prefix (e.g., '0000-00-00') that resolves to a non-existent or inaccessible directory, causing the archive write to fail silently and the record to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the groupByDay=false code path by injecting a stream file with a filename that causes the substring(0,10) call to produce a directory name that collides with an existing file (not a directory), causing an IOException that silently drops the record from the export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream filename with Unicode characters in the first 10 code units that, after Path.resolve(), produce a directory name that differs from the expected date-based folder, causing records to be archived in the wrong location and missing from the standard export path?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the network configuration value (e.g., via a crafted environment variable or config injection) to set network to 'demo-anything', causing getNetwork() to return 'demo' via StringUtils.substringBefore, which then triggers isAllowAnonymousAccess() to return true and routes the importer to the demo bucket, resulting in incorrect records being exported from the wrong network?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NETWORK_PREFIX_DELIMITER='-' parsing in getNetwork() by injecting a network value like 'mainnet-' (trailing delimiter) so that getNetwork() returns 'mainnet' but getNetworkPrefix() returns null (empty string after delimiter), causing the importer to select the mainnet bucket but skip prefix-based filtering, leading to incorrect records being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a network value containing multiple '-' delimiters (e.g., 'testnet-2023-01-extra') so that getNetworkPrefix() returns '2023-01-extra' (everything after the first '-'), causing the network prefix filter to match an overly broad or incorrect set of stream folders and ingest records from the wrong network prefix?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetworkPrefix()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network value to a string where the prefix after '-' is empty (e.g., 'testnet-') so that getNetworkPrefix() returns null, causing downstream components that rely on a non-null prefix to skip prefix-based record filtering and ingest records from all prefixes, resulting in incorrect records being exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: HederaNetwork.getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the network value resolves to 'other' (via getNetwork() parsing), causing getBucketName() to return an empty string '', which then causes the S3 provider to query an empty bucket name and either fail silently or fall back to a default bucket, resulting in missing records in the export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the network value to resolve to an unrecognized string (not in NETWORK_DEFAULT_BUCKETS), causing getBlockStreamBucketName() to return '' (empty string), which causes the block stream downloader to query an empty bucket name and silently skip all block stream records, resulting in missing block records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: HederaNetwork.hasCutover()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the network value to be something other than 'mainnet' or 'testnet' (e.g., 'previewnet' or 'other'), causing hasCutover() to return false and disabling the cutover logic, which results in the importer continuing to read from the old record stream bucket instead of the new block stream bucket, causing incorrect or stale records to be exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: HederaNetwork.isAllowAnonymousAccess()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isAllowAnonymousAccess() only returns true for 'demo', so that by crafting a network value that parses to 'demo' via getNetwork() (e.g., 'demo-suffix'), the importer enables anonymous access to the demo bucket and begins ingesting demo network records instead of the intended network's records, causing incorrect records to be exported?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external user set the network property to an empty string (bypassing @NotBlank if validation is not enforced at runtime) so that getNetwork() returns '' after toLowerCase(), causing NETWORK_DEFAULT_BUCKETS.get('') to return null and getBucketName() to return '', resulting in the importer connecting to no bucket and all records being missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: startDate)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that startDate has no @NotNull constraint and defaults to null, so that when the database is empty and blockstream is disabled and the network is not 'demo', the effective start date falls back to STARTUP_TIME, causing all historical records before startup to be permanently skipped and missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: endDate)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the endDate field defaulting to Utility.MAX_INSTANT_LONG (not truly Long.MAX_VALUE nanoseconds) so that if endDate is set to a value just beyond MAX_INSTANT_LONG, the DateRangeCalculator accepts it without error but downstream nanosecond conversion overflows, causing records near the boundary to be incorrectly filtered out and missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: endBlockNumber)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @PositiveOrZero constraint on endBlockNumber by setting it to 0, causing shouldGetBlock() to return false for all block numbers >= 1, effectively halting all block ingestion and resulting in all block-based records being missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: startBlockNumber)] Can an unprivileged external user set startBlockNumber to -1 (the minimum allowed by @Min(-1)), which maps to EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER in AbstractBlockSource, causing the block file source to throw an IllegalStateException ('doesn't support earliest available block number'), halting block ingestion and resulting in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: startBlockNumber)] Can an unprivileged external user set startBlockNumber to a very large positive value (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE) so that the importer skips all currently available blocks and waits indefinitely for a block that will never arrive, causing all existing records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: consensusMode)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where consensusMode is set to EQUAL (all nodes equally weighted) instead of STAKE_IN_ADDRESS_BOOK, causing nodes not in the address book to participate in consensus calculation, which may allow a minority of rogue nodes to reach consensus threshold and cause incorrect records to be accepted and exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: consensusMode)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the STAKE consensus mode (where all nodes specify their own stake) by influencing the address book to include nodes with inflated stake values, causing the consensus threshold to be met with fewer nodes and allowing incorrect stream files to be accepted and exported as valid records?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: groupByDay)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the groupByDay=true default so that when a stream file with a filename shorter than 10 characters is processed, getArchiveDestinationFolderPath() throws an unchecked exception that propagates up and causes the entire batch of records to be dropped, resulting in missing records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: groupByDay)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream filename whose first 10 characters are a date string that, when used as a directory name on the target filesystem, exceeds the maximum filename length or contains invalid characters, causing the archive write to fail and the record to be silently missing from the export?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: dataPath)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default dataPath of Paths.get('.', 'data') (a relative path) so that if the working directory changes between application start and record archival, the resolved streamPath points to a different location, causing archived records to be written to an unintended path and missing from the expected export location?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ImporterProperties (field: dataPath)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that dataPath has no path-traversal validation, so that if an attacker can influence the dataPath configuration (e.g., via environment variable injection), they can set it to a path like '/tmp/../../etc', causing streamPath to resolve to an unintended directory and records to be archived in the wrong location?
